title: Whack-a-drone
excerpt: Brendan Carr’s FCC keeps approving the very drones it was supposed to ban. How did it go so wrong?
content_html:
The US tried to ban Chinese drones. Brendan Carr can’t keep them grounded.

by Sean Hollister

Jul 24, 2026, 12:00 PM UTC

Sean Hollister

is a senior editor and founding member of The Verge who covers gadgets, games, and toys. He spent 15 years editing the likes of CNET, Gizmodo, and Engadget.

In Pasadena, California, there’s a cute red brick courtyard where one storefront isn’t like the rest. The glass doors open onto a sparse industrial hallway, which leads to a sunlit foyer with a large spiral staircase. Go up, and you’ll see a typical coworking space: open tables, healthy snacks, a collection of meeting rooms with glass walls. Follow one hallway most of the way back, and you’ll find Office 18.

There are no signs of life in this tiny private office that’s supposed to be the headquarters of Odyssey Robot, an upstart US drone company. We saw three small desks and chairs, but no power strips, no monitors, no keyboards or chargers or decorations. Two of the desks were covered with sealed cardboard packages.

This room is the latest proof: The FCC’s ban on foreign-made drones is a bad joke.

On December 22nd, 2025, the US government claimed — without a shred of public evidence — that foreign consumer drones posed an unacceptable threat to national security. With the help of other agencies, FCC Chairman Brendan Carr banned all future foreign drones from entering the country without a special new waiver.

Like with Carr’s make-it-make-sense consumer internet router ban, that waiver has nothing to do with the security of these products. The application includes zero questions about restricting or improving the technology itself.

Instead, it gives Carr and President Donald Trump the power to decide losers and winners while beating the “America First” drum. Foreign companies have to submit a detailed plan to build their products in the US, including how much they intend to invest in US manufacturing — yet US-based Netgear got a waiver from the router ban even though it made no commitment to produce devices outside Asia.

Consumer drones are effectively all made in China, and as of April, it appeared that no one planned to change that as a result of the FCC’s foreign drone ban. Though some US dronemakers exist, they’re pursuing a different and more lucrative opportunity: drones that spy and kill.

But Office 18 shows another gaping hole in the FCC’s plan. Instead of creating new US consumer drone companies, it’s incentivizing fake ones instead.

How easy is it to fool the FCC? Just lie on the paperwork, it seems.

Just whip up a new company that pretends to build brand-new drones in the USA and count on no one noticing — even if your drones clearly use proprietary DJI wireless tech.

DJI, the world’s biggest drone company, didn’t dominate drones just because of first-mover advantage. It was technology. DJI’s OcuSync and older Lightbridge real-time video transmission systems were and are the most reliable long-range, low-latency ways to remotely control a consumer-grade drone. Almost every time The Verge has tested another brand, the signal hasn’t been as reliable. It’s a competitive advantage that DJI wouldn’t easily share.

But OcuSync has a telltale fingerprint. It uses specific pairs of radio frequencies, pairs that appear in the FCC’s databases.

When software developer and journalist Konrad Iturbe began watching FCC databases for those frequencies, he realized that DJI was preparing to play a grand game of Whac-A-Mole in the United States. Well ahead of the December 2025 drone ban, a host of new companies had suddenly appeared selling barely disguised versions of DJI technology. He dubbed them “DJI front companies.”

One of the most prominent DJI clones to enter the US was Skyrover, which released a budget drone that was a dead ringer for the DJI Mini 4 Pro. Last July, DJI would not confirm or deny to The Verge that “Skyrover” was a disguised version of its own drone, even though security researchers confirmed its app included DJI code.

Then, in October, Iturbe helped us introduce you to Xtra, the company that lets DJI sneak its popular cameras into the US. When we tested an Xtra camera versus a DJI camera, we found them so identical, it removed any doubt. Again, DJI would not confirm or deny.

As a result, US citizens were still able to purchase DJI tech — only now, they were dealing with fly-by-night businesses, with no real guarantee of support in the future.

This June, Iturbe discovered the most brazen attempt yet. The DJI fingerprint led him to the FCC filings for a company named Odyssey Robot, which does business as Galiview Tech. In March, it became the first DJI-equipped drone brand to get FCC approval since the drone ban began. DJI did not reply to requests for comment for this story.

If Galiview was trying to hide the DJI connection, it didn’t try very hard: The company’s website even listed DJI’s trademarked OcuSync tech as a Galiview feature.

Wasn’t stopping clandestine Chinese hardware the whole point of the ban? How could the FCC approve this drone?

Simple: On February 6th, a “Randolph Howard Eason” declared on an FCC form that Odyssey Robot’s drone and controller were made in the United States, and nobody ever questioned that before rubber-stamping its application.

The FCC could have spotted the OcuSync frequencies, or that this supposedly US-manufactured gadget was tested by a Chinese lab, or that it used a Chinese-made antenna also used by another Chinese drone.

But it’s easier than that. Odyssey Robot declared that its drone was designed, developed, and manufactured at 21 Miller Alley Suite 210 in Pasadena, California. Even a basic Google search can show you that’s not the address of a factory — it’s a coworking space called Industrious that explicitly prohibits members from manufacturing anything onsite.

Odyssey Robot also declared that its drones are assembled at eTak Worldwide Corporation in Grand Prairie, Texas. With 80,000 square feet of warehouse space and 15 loading docks, you could theoretically build drones there. But again, a basic Google search would show you that eTak isn’t an assembler; it’s a recycling company that collects e-waste, including old batteries, then sorts and dismantles them.

Still, we wanted to be sure. We called up the receptionist at the coworking space in Pasadena; they confirmed that Odyssey Robot was actively renting a two-person private office. Official California business records claimed the company was operating out of Office 18. You know the rest: We found a lonely room with three unused desks topped with cardboard packages.

If the FCC had ever seen the website for these drones, it could have found even more eyebrow-raising contradictions. The website claimed that “Galiview Inc.” was founded in 2022 and headquartered in San Francisco, even as it provided a Las Vegas phone number and a Pasadena address.

“Based in the heart of San Francisco’s technology innovation hub, we’ve been pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in consumer drone technology,” it claimed.

In reality, public records show that Galiview is not a corporation. It’s a trademark of Odyssey Robot, LLC, which was formed in Delaware in January 2025 and registered in California last December, after what seems like an aborted attempt to call the brand “Flyon” instead. Other trademark filings from February 2026 show it planning at least three products: a Galiview Titan, Galiview Venus, and Galiview Nova.

The company never replied to my emails to contact@flyondrone.com, the one and only email address it has provided publicly. We found its only phone number disconnected: “The subscriber you have dialed is not in service.”

eTak Worldwide, the recycler, also wasn’t answering emails or picking up the phone. “Sorry, but the user’s mailbox can’t accept any more messages,” I heard time and again. We had no better luck with “iNet Expert,” an earlier company from eTak’s founder that claimed to offer wholesale computer parts. Every time I tried the company’s phone tree, the answer was always a robotic “No one is available.”

But when I realized iNet Expert was just 30 minutes from my house, I decided to pay it a visit.

The building was locked, but through the window I saw something strange. The primary decoration was a nearly floor-to-ceiling marketing image for a smartphone screen protector from 2014, from a company called Rudaor. It appears that iNet tried to become a US distributor, judging by the roughly 300 views on YouTube. On the windows of iNet’s office, there were also signs for Hirutech USA, the defunct US arm of a Taiwanese electronics business development firm that once occupied the same address.

1/3

The whole building was a time capsule — and yet, it’s still getting a few shipments from overseas, including on July 15th, according to import records collected by ImportYeti.

eTak’s founder has a history of lending out his business address, I thought.

But when I finally stumble across a cell phone number for Richard Lee, founder of eTak and iNet Expert, he says he’d never heard of Odyssey Robot until a week or so earlier.

“I’m happy you called me, because we totally have no idea what’s going on,” Lee told me on July 13th. “Why are people using our company name to do this kind of thing? It’s kind of ridiculous.”

When I show him Odyssey Robot’s sworn attestation that eTak assembles its drones, he says it must have picked his address out of a search engine.

“No, we don’t assemble anything, we only refurbish,” he says when I ask, adding that eTak doesn’t have the manpower. He says he’s recycled batteries for Taiwanese computer maker Wistron (which builds laptops for giants like HP and Dell), UV ink cartridges for Anker, and vacuums for Ecovacs, but he’s never had a relationship with dronemaker DJI.

“They are a huge company, they will not choose us,” he says. “If they use our company name, it’s not fair for us, it’s not right.”

But whoever’s behind Odyssey Robot is likely to get away with the lies.

Since 2023, every foreign company selling wireless gadgets in the United States has theoretically needed an extra human in the loop, based in the United States. That February is when the FCC added the requirement for a “US agent” who it can legally serve papers to, and communicate with, so the FCC can enforce against a company that might be unreachable under international law.

But there’s nothing in the rules that requires anyone to verify the US agent is a known, reputable person with a working telephone number and email address, working out of an actual permanent office. And though the FCC might have guidelines suggesting those things, Keller & Heckman partner Greg Kunkle tells The Verge that it’s unlikely anyone’s bothering to check.

“To my knowledge, there’s no real vetting of the US agent,” he tells The Verge. “If you’re making a misstatement, that’s a rule violation, but I don’t think the FCC or TCB is checking those actively. I’m not sure anybody is.”

Kunkle says that for the past decade or so, FCC authorizations haven’t actually been handled by the FCC at all — the agency outsourced that role to roughly 40 private companies called TCBs, short for Telecommunications Certification Bodies. “Once the TCB finishes its process, you’re good to go,” says Kunkle, adding that those authorizations go straight into the FCC database with no more FCC input at all.

A TCB could theoretically get in trouble for letting banned companies through, even lose its status — and the FCC does ask TCBs not to take the “US agent” at their word. The agency added specific guidelines this past December about that, including the requirement for “a valid physical US mailing address,” not just a virtual mailbox or a vacant lot:

“TCBs should not rely solely on the applicant’s certification,” the FCC writes. If a TCB has any reason to believe that a gadget may be banned, the FCC says it shouldn’t process the application at all. And if a TCB decides it did the wrong thing, it should “immediately submit a KDB inquiry explaining the issue to the FCC,” the agency adds.

But does that happen? We reached out to TÜV Rheinland, the TCB that approved Odyssey Robot’s FCC authorization, to let it know we were writing a story about Odyssey Robot making potentially fraudulent attestations, and the company still couldn’t find anything wrong.

“As of today, we do not have any concrete evidence for questioning our certifications to Odyssey Robot. If there is a reasonable basis for questioning a certification, we will follow the procedures established by the FCC for such cases,” TÜV Rheinland communications manager Maximilian Grün told The Verge on July 15th.

TÜV Rheinland said it hadn’t received any communication from the FCC regarding Odyssey Robot, but also that Odyssey Robot did not forge the TÜV Rheinland test reports attached to its FCC filing. Those were genuine.

If the TCBs do their jobs, it’s possible they would catch some fake brands. The TCBs are supposed to audit 5 percent of all devices they certify in a calendar year and report back to the FCC with their findings — including whether a company fails to provide a sample of a product for testing. Perhaps Odyssey Robot would have been the 1 in 20 and get caught that way.

But so far, the only “DJI front companies” we’ve seen targeted by the FCC are the ones that journalists brought to the agency’s attention.

On July 10th, the FCC proposed $25,000 fines against eight of the “DJI front companies,” noting that they were alerted to the brands through research from Konrad Iturbe and my own coverage on The Verge. It justified the fines, and possibly further action, because those companies’ US agents didn’t even bother replying to the agency’s letters.

In 2024, the FCC similarly proposed over $700,000 in fines against Eken, a Hong Kong-based video doorbell manufacturer that also failed to provide working contact information for a US agent. That time, it was journalists at Consumer Reports who prompted the FCC to act.

But even if the FCC can issue a fine, it probably won’t be able to collect.

Earlier this year, the agency cited the Eken case as evidence that “foreign actors in our equipment authorization have been able to evade US law when products they are responsible for have harmed American consumers.” Even if a company pretends to be based in the US, the FCC can’t reach them if they’re actually in China instead.

There might still be real financial consequences for some DJI front companies. On July 17th, the FCC took things up a notch by threatening to retroactively ban the DJI fronts’ already-approved products from import, sale, marketing, and distribution, which could be the end for successful brands like Xtra and Skyrover that snuck in before the ban.

But it seems like that might be where the FCC’s direct powers of enforcement end. Former FCC enforcement chief Peter Hyun tells The Verge that the agency will want to send a message to other companies not to do this thing, but the tools it has in its tool belt are forfeitures, cease and desist letters, and “show-cause” orders where a company has to explain why its license shouldn’t be revoked. The FCC could also theoretically help the Department of Justice bring a smuggling charge, he says.

Lying smugglers hiding behind disconnected phone numbers won’t be stepping forward to provide their real identities, of course.

On July 21st, a month and a half after Iturbe and The Verge asked the FCC about Odyssey Robot for this story, the agency revealed it had sent a “show-cause” order to the company a few days after we tipped them off. The FCC’s investigation was limited to sending letters to Odyssey and eTak and noting whether the two entities responded. Odyssey didn’t respond. eTak denied any relationship with Odyssey.

eTak replied to the FCC on July 13th, the same day I spoke to its founder on the phone.

The question now isn’t what happens to the empty husk of Odyssey Robot, but how many other smugglers might sneak through when the agency isn’t tipped off. If the only reason the FCC caught Odyssey is a guy named Konrad — well, that’s not how government agencies are supposed to work.

The FCC’s consumer gadget bans are broken if they don’t require anyone to do a basic Google search before legally approving banned gadgets for import and sale. They’re broken if the FCC can’t track down and make a meaningful example of a bad actor even after they’re caught, because the same lies that made them invisible also shield them from consequences.

Since Odyssey hadn’t yet begun selling drones in the US, revoking its FCC authorization likely wouldn’t matter to its mystery owners. It wouldn’t be surprising if their takeaway is to lie more carefully, rather than not lying at all.

But it does seem that the FCC is attempting to close some loopholes.

At first, it seemed that the FCC was following the America First formula of punishing Chinese entities based on their affiliation rather than their actions — it took action against eight so-called “bad labs” that test radios in China simply because Chinese state-sponsored entities had more than 10 percent equity. When it moved against SGS-CSTC Shenzhen, a lab used by at least one DJI front, it didn’t mention that front in its justification for the move.

But on July 21st, the FCC moved against test lab Shenzhen STS because it “willfully provided false test results” for a variety of Bluetooth headsets, phones, and tablets, which the lab blamed on negligent staff. Perhaps the FCC will act against TCBs and test labs that aren’t carefully checking banned gadgets, too.

The agency is also now proposing to make online retailers like Amazon display the FCC IDs of each device on their product pages so that consumers can check for themselves whether the names line up, at least. Amazon already collects those FCC IDs from sellers, but spokesperson Juliana Karber confirms the company’s never required them to actually display the IDs publicly.

The FCC did not respond to any of our repeated requests for comment over the past two months.

So far, we’ve been unable to track down “Randolph Howard Eason,” the “compliance director” who submitted Odyssey Robot’s paperwork. No one we’ve found by that name or similar seems likely to be a fit, and those who might have been paid by Odyssey — like the coworking space and TÜV Rheinland — declined to tell The Verge anything more about their clients.

Not that we’d necessarily get very far, because Odyssey Robot used middlemen to cover its tracks. For example, Sean Hartlieb, an attorney in Hawaii who processed the “Galiview” trademark, tells The Verge that he never interacted with the company at all. He says he processes trademark filings for all sorts of Chinese companies as a side gig, getting his assignments from a Chinese firm that charges a flat fee for US trademarks.

By paying small standardized fees, it might not be that expensive for a big company to build lots of shell companies and see which ones escape notice. “$20,000, maybe something like that?” Kunkle suggests each might cost. “Trademarks, TCBs, test labs, it’s probably somewhere in that range. It’s not much more than that.”

So what keeps a big foreign brand from sneaking through?

“I hate to say it, but it would be pretty easy,” says Kunkle. “I think just the volume of devices coming into the United States — if you’re the type of person that’s willing to fudge this paperwork and you reside in some place where you think you’re unreachable by the US government, I don’t think this would be hard to fake at all.”

Image: Alex Parkin / The Verge, FCC, Skyrover

If anyone were to make a definitive link between Odyssey Robot and DJI, I thought it would be one of the firms that registered its LLC in the United States. Odyssey Robot is currently registered through Delaware Business Incorporators and California Registered Agent. But Delaware is a state that lets companies set up LLCs anonymously.

“Unfortunately, it opens up the door to fraud and there’s not much recourse,” says the receptionist at Delaware Business Incorporators.

If I want to reach Odyssey Robot, the receptionist at California Registered Agent tells me, I can try mailing the message to her office in Sacramento instead.

“We’re real!” she says. “Sometimes our clients are not, unfortunately.”

Additional reporting by John Higgins.

Perhaps Odyssey Robot would have been the 1 in 20 and get caught that way.But so far, the only “DJI front companies” we’ve seen targeted by the FCC are the ones that journalists brought to the agency’s attention.On July 10th, the FCC proposed $25,000 fines against eight of the “DJI front companies,” noting that they were alerted to the brands through research from Konrad Iturbe and my own coverage on The Verge. It justified the fines, and possibly further action, because those companies’ US agents didn’t even bother replying to the agency’s letters.In 2024, the FCC similarly proposed over $700,000 in fines against Eken, a Hong Kong-based video doorbell manufacturer that also failed to provide working contact information for a US agent. That time, it was journalists at Consumer Reports who prompted the FCC to act.But even if the FCC can issue a fine, it probably won’t be able to collect.Earlier this year, the agency cited the Eken case as evidence that “foreign actors in our equipment authorization have been able to evade US law when products they are responsible for have harmed American consumers.” Even if a company pretends to be based in the US, the FCC can’t reach them if they’re actually in China instead.There might still be real financial consequences for some DJI front companies. On July 17th, the FCC took things up a notch by threatening to retroactively ban the DJI fronts’ already-approved products from import, sale, marketing, and distribution, which could be the end for successful brands like Xtra and Skyrover that snuck in before the ban.But it seems like that might be where the FCC’s direct powers of enforcement end. Former FCC enforcement chief Peter Hyun tells The Verge that the agency will want to send a message to other companies not to do this thing, but the tools it has in its tool belt are forfeitures, cease and desist letters, and “show-cause” orders where a company has to explain why its license shouldn’t be revoked. The FCC could also theoretically help the Department of Justice bring a smuggling charge, he says.Lying smugglers hiding behind disconnected phone numbers won’t be stepping forward to provide their real identities, of course.On July 21st, a month and a half after Iturbe and The Verge asked the FCC about Odyssey Robot for this story, the agency revealed it had sent a “show-cause” order to the company a few days after we tipped them off. The FCC’s investigation was limited to sending letters to Odyssey and eTak and noting whether the two entities responded. Odyssey didn’t respond. eTak denied any relationship with Odyssey.eTak replied to the FCC on July 13th, the same day I spoke to its founder on the phone.The question now isn’t what happens to the empty husk of Odyssey Robot, but how many other smugglers might sneak through when the agency isn’t tipped off. If the only reason the FCC caught Odyssey is a guy named Konrad — well, that’s not how government agencies are supposed to work.The FCC’s consumer gadget bans are broken if they don’t require anyone to do a basic Google search before legally approving banned gadgets for import and sale. They’re broken if the FCC can’t track down and make a meaningful example of a bad actor even after they’re caught, because the same lies that made them invisible also shield them from consequences.Since Odyssey hadn’t yet begun selling drones in the US, revoking its FCC authorization likely wouldn’t matter to its mystery owners. It wouldn’t be surprising if their takeaway is to lie more carefully, rather than not lying at all.But it does seem that the FCC is attempting to close some loopholes.At first, it seemed that the FCC was following the America First formula of punishing Chinese entities based on their affiliation rather than their actions — it took action against eight so-called “bad labs” that test radios in China simply because Chinese state-sponsored entities had more than 10 percent equity. When it moved against SGS-CSTC Shenzhen, a lab used by at least one DJI front, it didn’t mention that front in its justification for the move.But on July 21st, the FCC moved against test lab Shenzhen STS because it “willfully provided false test results” for a variety of Bluetooth headsets, phones, and tablets, which the lab blamed on negligent staff. Perhaps the FCC will act against TCBs and test labs that aren’t carefully checking banned gadgets, too.The agency is also now proposing to make online retailers like Amazon display the FCC IDs of each device on their product pages so that consumers can check for themselves whether the names line up, at least. Amazon already collects those FCC IDs from sellers, but spokesperson Juliana Karber confirms the company’s never required them to actually display the IDs publicly.The FCC did not respond to any of our repeated requests for comment over the past two months.TÜV Rheinland is the TCB that processed Odyssey Robot’s paperwork. So far, we’ve been unable to track down “Randolph Howard Eason,” the “compliance director” who submitted Odyssey Robot’s paperwork. No one we’ve found by that name or similar seems likely to be a fit, and those who might have been paid by Odyssey — like the coworking space and TÜV Rheinland — declined to tell The Verge anything more about their clients.Not that we’d necessarily get very far, because Odyssey Robot used middlemen to cover its tracks. For example, Sean Hartlieb, an attorney in Hawaii who processed the “Galiview” trademark, tells The Verge that he never interacted with the company at all. He says he processes trademark filings for all sorts of Chinese companies as a side gig, getting his assignments from a Chinese firm that charges a flat fee for US trademarks.By paying small standardized fees, it might not be that expensive for a big company to build lots of shell companies and see which ones escape notice. “$20,000, maybe something like that?” Kunkle suggests each might cost. “Trademarks, TCBs, test labs, it’s probably somewhere in that range. It’s not much more than that.”So what keeps a big foreign brand from sneaking through?“I hate to say it, but it would be pretty easy,” says Kunkle. “I think just the volume of devices coming into the United States — if you’re the type of person that’s willing to fudge this paperwork and you reside in some place where you think you’re unreachable by the US government, I don’t think this would be hard to fake at all.”Image: Alex Parkin / The Verge, FCC, SkyroverIf anyone were to make a definitive link between Odyssey Robot and DJI, I thought it would be one of the firms that registered its LLC in the United States. Odyssey Robot is currently registered through Delaware Business Incorporators and California Registered Agent. But Delaware is a state that lets companies set up LLCs anonymously.“Unfortunately, it opens up the door to fraud and there’s not much recourse,” says the receptionist at Delaware Business Incorporators.If I want to reach Odyssey Robot, the receptionist at California Registered Agent tells me, I can try mailing the message to her office in Sacramento instead.“We’re real!” she says. “Sometimes our clients are not, unfortunately.”Additional reporting by John Higgins. Follow topics and authors from this story to see more like this in your personalized homepage feed and to receive email updates.Sean Hollister

blocks.html_en: In Pasadena, California, there’s a cute red brick courtyard where one storefront isn’t like the rest. The glass doors open onto a sparse industrial hallway, which leads to a sunlit foyer with a large spiral staircase. Go up, and you’ll see a typical coworking space: open tables, healthy snacks, a collection of meeting rooms with glass walls. Follow one hallway most of the way back, and you’ll find Office 18.

sentences.en: In Pasadena, California, there’s a cute red brick courtyard where one storefront isn’t like the rest.
sentences.zh: 在加利福尼亚州帕萨迪纳，有一个可爱的红砖庭院，其中一个店面与其他店面不同。

sentences.en: The glass doors open onto a sparse industrial hallway, which leads to a sunlit foyer with a large spiral staircase.
sentences.zh: 玻璃门通向一条稀疏的工业走廊，通向阳光明媚的门厅，门厅设有大型螺旋楼梯。

sentences.en: Go up, and you’ll see a typical coworking space: open tables, healthy snacks, a collection of meeting rooms with glass walls.
sentences.zh: 上去，你会看到一个典型的联合办公空间：开放的桌子、健康的零食、一系列带有玻璃墙的会议室。

sentences.en: Follow one hallway most of the way back, and you’ll find Office 18.
sentences.zh: 沿着一条走廊走完大部分路程，您就会找到 Office 18。

blocks.html_en: There are no signs of life in this tiny private office that’s supposed to be the headquarters of Odyssey Robot, an upstart US drone company. We saw three small desks and chairs, but no power strips, no monitors, no keyboards or chargers or decorations. Two of the desks were covered with sealed cardboard packages.

sentences.en: There are no signs of life in this tiny private office that’s supposed to be the headquarters of Odyssey Robot, an upstart US drone company.
sentences.zh: 这间狭小的私人办公室本应是美国无人机公司奥德赛机器人的总部，但没有任何生命迹象。

sentences.en: We saw three small desks and chairs, but no power strips, no monitors, no keyboards or chargers or decorations.
sentences.zh: 我们看到了三张小桌椅，但没有电源板，没有显示器，没有键盘，没有充电器，也没有装饰品。

sentences.en: Two of the desks were covered with sealed cardboard packages.
sentences.zh: 其中两张桌子上覆盖着密封的纸板包装。

blocks.html_en: This room is the latest proof: The FCC’s ban on foreign-made drones is a bad joke.

sentences.en: This room is the latest proof: The FCC’s ban on foreign-made drones is a bad joke.
sentences.zh: 这个房间就是最新的证据：FCC 对外国制造的无人机的禁令是一个糟糕的笑话。

sentences.en: This is where Odyssey Robot claimed to be manufacturing drones.
sentences.zh: 这是奥德赛机器人声称制造无人机的地方。

blocks.html_en: On December 22nd, 2025, the US government claimed — without a shred of public evidence — that foreign consumer drones posed an unacceptable threat to national security. With the help of other agencies, FCC Chairman Brendan Carr banned all future foreign drones from entering the country without a special new waiver.

sentences.en: On December 22nd, 2025, the US government claimed — without a shred of public evidence — that foreign consumer drones posed an unacceptable threat to national security.
sentences.zh: 2025 年 12 月 22 日，美国政府在没有任何公开证据的情况下声称，外国消费级无人机对国家安全构成了不可接受的威胁。

sentences.en: With the help of other agencies, FCC Chairman Brendan Carr banned all future foreign drones from entering the country without a special new waiver.
sentences.zh: 在其他机构的帮助下，联邦通信委员会主席布伦丹·卡尔禁止未来所有外国无人机进入该国，而无需获得特别的新豁免。

blocks.html_en: Like with Carr’s make-it-make-sense consumer internet router ban, that waiver has nothing to do with the security of these products. The application includes zero questions about restricting or improving the technology itself.

sentences.en: Like with Carr’s make-it-make-sense consumer internet router ban, that waiver has nothing to do with the security of these products.
sentences.zh: 就像卡尔提出的有意义的消费者互联网路由器禁令一样，这项豁免与这些产品的安全性无关。

sentences.en: The application includes zero questions about restricting or improving the technology itself.
sentences.zh: 该应用程序包括关于限制或改进技术本身的零问题。

blocks.html_en: Instead, it gives Carr and President Donald Trump the power to decide losers and winners while beating the “America First” drum. Foreign companies have to submit a detailed plan to build their products in the US, including how much they intend to invest in US manufacturing — yet US-based Netgear got a waiver from the router ban even though it made no commitment to produce devices outside Asia.

sentences.en: Instead, it gives Carr and President Donald Trump the power to decide losers and winners while beating the “America First” drum.
sentences.zh: 相反，它赋予卡尔和唐纳德·特朗普总统决定输家和赢家的权力，同时敲响“美国优先”的鼓点。

sentences.en: Foreign companies have to submit a detailed plan to build their products in the US, including how much they intend to invest in US manufacturing — yet US-based Netgear got a waiver from the router ban even though it made no commitment to produce devices outside Asia.
sentences.zh: 外国公司必须提交一份在美国生产产品的详细计划，包括他们打算在美国制造业投资多少——然而，总部位于美国的 Netgear 获得了路由器禁令的豁免，尽管它没有承诺在亚洲以外生产设备。

blocks.html_en: Consumer drones are effectively all made in China, and as of April, it appeared that no one planned to change that as a result of the FCC’s foreign drone ban. Though some US dronemakers exist, they’re pursuing a different and more lucrative opportunity: drones that spy and kill.

sentences.en: Consumer drones are effectively all made in China, and as of April, it appeared that no one planned to change that as a result of the FCC’s foreign drone ban.
sentences.zh: 消费级无人机实际上全部在中国制造，截至 4 月份，似乎没有人计划因 FCC 的外国无人机禁令而改变这一现状。

sentences.en: Though some US dronemakers exist, they’re pursuing a different and more lucrative opportunity: drones that spy and kill.
sentences.zh: 尽管存在一些美国无人机制造商，但他们正在追求一种不同的、更有利可图的机会：侦察和杀戮的无人机。

blocks.html_en: But Office 18 shows another gaping hole in the FCC’s plan. Instead of creating new US consumer drone companies, it’s incentivizing fake ones instead.

sentences.en: But Office 18 shows another gaping hole in the FCC’s plan.
sentences.zh: 但 Office 18 显示了 FCC 计划中的另一个漏洞。

sentences.en: Instead of creating new US consumer drone companies, it’s incentivizing fake ones instead.
sentences.zh: 它没有创建新的美国消费无人机公司，而是激励假冒公司。

blocks.html_en: How easy is it to fool the FCC? Just lie on the paperwork, it seems.

sentences.en: How easy is it to fool the FCC?
sentences.zh: 欺骗 FCC 有多容易？

sentences.en: Just lie on the paperwork, it seems.
sentences.zh: 看起来只是在文书工作上撒谎。

blocks.html_en: Just whip up a new company that pretends to build brand-new drones in the USA and count on no one noticing — even if your drones clearly use proprietary DJI wireless tech.

sentences.en: Just whip up a new company that pretends to build brand-new drones in the USA and count on no one noticing — even if your drones clearly use proprietary DJI wireless tech.
sentences.zh: 只要创办一家新公司，假装在美国制造全新的无人机，并且指望没有人注意到——即使你的无人机明显使用专有的 DJI 无线技术。

blocks.html_en: DJI, the world’s biggest drone company, didn’t dominate drones just because of first-mover advantage. It was technology. DJI’s OcuSync and older Lightbridge real-time video transmission systems were and are the most reliable long-range, low-latency ways to remotely control a consumer-grade drone. Almost every time The Verge has tested another brand, the signal hasn’t been as reliable. It’s a competitive advantage that DJI wouldn’t easily share.

sentences.en: DJI, the world’s biggest drone company, didn’t dominate drones just because of first-mover advantage.
sentences.zh: 全球最大的无人机公司大疆创新并不仅仅因为先发优势而在无人机领域占据主导地位。

sentences.en: It was technology.
sentences.zh: 这是技术。

sentences.en: DJI’s OcuSync and older Lightbridge real-time video transmission systems were and are the most reliable long-range, low-latency ways to remotely control a consumer-grade drone.
sentences.zh: DJI 的 OcuSync 和较旧的 Lightbridge 实时视频传输系统过去和现在都是远程控制消费级无人机的最可靠的远程、低延迟方式。

sentences.en: Almost every time The Verge has tested another brand, the signal hasn’t been as reliable.
sentences.zh: 几乎每次 The Verge 测试另一个品牌时，信号都不太可靠。

sentences.en: It’s a competitive advantage that DJI wouldn’t easily share.
sentences.zh: 这是大疆不会轻易分享的竞争优势。

blocks.html_en: But OcuSync has a telltale fingerprint. It uses specific pairs of radio frequencies, pairs that appear in the FCC’s databases.

sentences.en: But OcuSync has a telltale fingerprint.
sentences.zh: 但 OcuSync 有一个明显的指纹。

sentences.en: It uses specific pairs of radio frequencies, pairs that appear in the FCC’s databases.
sentences.zh: 它使用特定的无线电频率对，这些频率对出现在 FCC 的数据库中。

blocks.html_en: When software developer and journalist Konrad Iturbe began watching FCC databases for those frequencies, he realized that DJI was preparing to play a grand game of Whac-A-Mole in the United States. Well ahead of the December 2025 drone ban, a host of new companies had suddenly appeared selling barely disguised versions of DJI technology. He dubbed them “DJI front companies.”

sentences.en: When software developer and journalist Konrad Iturbe began watching FCC databases for those frequencies, he realized that DJI was preparing to play a grand game of Whac-A-Mole in the United States.
sentences.zh: 当软件开发人员兼记者 Konrad Iturbe 开始查看 FCC 数据库中的这些频率时，他意识到 DJI 正准备在美国玩一场盛大的打地鼠游戏。

sentences.en: Well ahead of the December 2025 drone ban, a host of new companies had suddenly appeared selling barely disguised versions of DJI technology.
sentences.zh: 早在 2025 年 12 月无人机禁令出台之前，许多新公司突然出现，销售几乎不加掩饰的 DJI 技术版本。

sentences.en: He dubbed them “DJI front companies.”
sentences.zh: 他称它们为“大疆幌子公司”。

blocks.html_en: One of the most prominent DJI clones to enter the US was Skyrover, which released a budget drone that was a dead ringer for the DJI Mini 4 Pro. Last July, DJI would not confirm or deny to The Verge that “Skyrover” was a disguised version of its own drone, even though security researchers confirmed its app included DJI code.

sentences.en: One of the most prominent DJI clones to enter the US was Skyrover, which released a budget drone that was a dead ringer for the DJI Mini 4 Pro.
sentences.zh: Skyrover 是进入美国的最著名的 DJI 克隆产品之一，该公司发布了一款与 DJI Mini 4 Pro 非常相似的廉价无人机。

sentences.en: Last July, DJI would not confirm or deny to The Verge that “Skyrover” was a disguised version of its own drone, even though security researchers confirmed its app included DJI code.
sentences.zh: 去年 7 月，DJI 没有向 The Verge 证实或否认“Skyrover”是其自己无人机的伪装版本，尽管安全研究人员证实其应用程序包含 DJI 代码。

blocks.html_en: Then, in October, Iturbe helped us introduce you to Xtra, the company that lets DJI sneak its popular cameras into the US. When we tested an Xtra camera versus a DJI camera, we found them so identical, it removed any doubt. Again, DJI would not confirm or deny.

sentences.en: Then, in October, Iturbe helped us introduce you to Xtra, the company that lets DJI sneak its popular cameras into the US.
sentences.zh: 然后，在 10 月份，Iturbe 帮助我们向您介绍了 Xtra，这家公司让 DJI 将其受欢迎的相机偷偷进入美国。

sentences.en: When we tested an Xtra camera versus a DJI camera, we found them so identical, it removed any doubt.
sentences.zh: 当我们测试 Xtra 相机与 DJI 相机时，我们发现它们非常相似，这消除了任何疑虑。

sentences.en: Again, DJI would not confirm or deny.
sentences.zh: 再次强调，大疆创新既没有证实也没有否认。

blocks.html_en: As a result, US citizens were still able to purchase DJI tech — only now, they were dealing with fly-by-night businesses, with no real guarantee of support in the future.

sentences.en: As a result, US citizens were still able to purchase DJI tech — only now, they were dealing with fly-by-night businesses, with no real guarantee of support in the future.
sentences.zh: 结果，美国公民仍然能够购买大疆科技——只是现在，他们正在处理不可靠的业务，未来没有真正的支持保证。

blocks.html_en: This June, Iturbe discovered the most brazen attempt yet. The DJI fingerprint led him to the FCC filings for a company named Odyssey Robot, which does business as Galiview Tech. In March, it became the first DJI-equipped drone brand to get FCC approval since the drone ban began. DJI did not reply to requests for comment for this story.

sentences.en: This June, Iturbe discovered the most brazen attempt yet.
sentences.zh: 今年六月，伊图尔韦发现了迄今为止最无耻的尝试。

sentences.en: The DJI fingerprint led him to the FCC filings for a company named Odyssey Robot, which does business as Galiview Tech.
sentences.zh: DJI 指纹让他找到了一家名为 Odyssey Robot 的公司向 FCC 提交的文件，该公司的业务名称为 Galiview Tech。

sentences.en: In March, it became the first DJI-equipped drone brand to get FCC approval since the drone ban began.
sentences.zh: 今年3月，它成为自无人机禁令开始以来第一个获得FCC批准的配备DJI的无人机品牌。

sentences.en: DJI did not reply to requests for comment for this story.
sentences.zh: 大疆创新没有回复对此报道的置评请求。

blocks.html_en: If Galiview was trying to hide the DJI connection, it didn’t try very hard: The company’s website even listed DJI’s trademarked OcuSync tech as a Galiview feature.

sentences.en: If Galiview was trying to hide the DJI connection, it didn’t try very hard: The company’s website even listed DJI’s trademarked OcuSync tech as a Galiview feature.
sentences.zh: 如果说 Galiview 试图隐藏 DJI 连接，那么它并没有非常努力：该公司的网站甚至将 DJI 的商标 OcuSync 技术列为 Galiview 功能。

sentences.en: Galiview’s website claimed that its drone would have OcuSync, among other DJI trademarks and marketing phrases.
sentences.zh: Galiview 的网站声称，其无人机将拥有 OcuSync 以及其他 DJI 商标和营销短语。

blocks.html_en: Wasn’t stopping clandestine Chinese hardware the whole point of the ban? How could the FCC approve this drone?

sentences.en: Wasn’t stopping clandestine Chinese hardware the whole point of the ban?
sentences.zh: 阻止秘密的中国硬件难道不是禁令的全部目的吗？

sentences.en: How could the FCC approve this drone?
sentences.zh: FCC 如何批准这款无人机？

blocks.html_en: Simple: On February 6th, a “Randolph Howard Eason” declared on an FCC form that Odyssey Robot’s drone and controller were made in the United States, and nobody ever questioned that before rubber-stamping its application.

sentences.en: Simple: On February 6th, a “Randolph Howard Eason” declared on an FCC form that Odyssey Robot’s drone and controller were made in the United States, and nobody ever questioned that before rubber-stamping its application.
sentences.zh: 很简单：2 月 6 日，“Randolph Howard Eason”在 FCC 表格上宣称，Odyssey Robot 的无人机和控制器是在美国制造的，在批准其申请之前，没有人质疑这一点。

sentences.en: This is Odyssey Robot’s declaration that its drone was made in the USA.
sentences.zh: 这是奥德赛机器人声明其无人机是在美国制造的。

blocks.html_en: The FCC could have spotted the OcuSync frequencies, or that this supposedly US-manufactured gadget was tested by a Chinese lab, or that it used a Chinese-made antenna also used by another Chinese drone.

sentences.en: The FCC could have spotted the OcuSync frequencies, or that this supposedly US-manufactured gadget was tested by a Chinese lab, or that it used a Chinese-made antenna also used by another Chinese drone.
sentences.zh: FCC 可能发现了 OcuSync 频率，或者这个据称是美国制造的小工具由中国实验室进行了测试，或者它使用了另一架中国无人机也使用的中国制造的天线。

blocks.html_en: But it’s easier than that. Odyssey Robot declared that its drone was designed, developed, and manufactured at 21 Miller Alley Suite 210 in Pasadena, California. Even a basic Google search can show you that’s not the address of a factory — it’s a coworking space called Industrious that explicitly prohibits members from manufacturing anything onsite.

sentences.en: But it’s easier than that.
sentences.zh: 但这比那更容易。

sentences.en: Odyssey Robot declared that its drone was designed, developed, and manufactured at 21 Miller Alley Suite 210 in Pasadena, California.
sentences.zh: 奥德赛机器人宣称，其无人机是在加利福尼亚州帕萨迪纳市米勒巷21号210室设计、开发和制造的。

sentences.en: Even a basic Google search can show you that’s not the address of a factory — it’s a coworking space called Industrious that explicitly prohibits members from manufacturing anything onsite.
sentences.zh: 即使是基本的谷歌搜索也可以告诉你这不是一家工厂的地址——这是一个名为“Industrious”的联合办公空间，明确禁止成员在现场制造任何东西。

blocks.html_en: Since 2023, every foreign company selling wireless gadgets in the United States has theoretically needed an extra human in the loop, based in the United States. That February is when the FCC added the requirement for a “US agent” who it can legally serve papers to, and communicate with, so the FCC can enforce against a company that might be unreachable under international law.

sentences.en: Since 2023, every foreign company selling wireless gadgets in the United States has theoretically needed an extra human in the loop, based in the United States.
sentences.zh: 自 2023 年以来，理论上每家在美国销售无线设备的外国公司都需要一名驻扎在美国的额外人员参与其中。

sentences.en: That February is when the FCC added the requirement for a “US agent” who it can legally serve papers to, and communicate with, so the FCC can enforce against a company that might be unreachable under international law.
sentences.zh: 那年 2 月，FCC 增加了对“美国代理人”的要求，FCC 可以合法地向其送达文件并与其进行沟通，这样 FCC 就可以对根据国际法可能无法联系到的公司采取强制措施。

blocks.html_en: But there’s nothing in the rules that requires anyone to verify the US agent is a known, reputable person with a working telephone number and email address, working out of an actual permanent office. And though the FCC might have guidelines suggesting those things, Keller & Heckman partner Greg Kunkle tells The Verge that it’s unlikely anyone’s bothering to check.

sentences.en: But there’s nothing in the rules that requires anyone to verify the US agent is a known, reputable person with a working telephone number and email address, working out of an actual permanent office.
sentences.zh: 但规则中没有任何内容要求任何人验证美国特工是一位知名的、信誉良好的人，拥有工作电话号码和电子邮件地址，在实际的常设办公室工作。

sentences.en: And though the FCC might have guidelines suggesting those things, Keller & Heckman partner Greg Kunkle tells The Verge that it’s unlikely anyone’s bothering to check.
sentences.zh: 尽管 FCC 可能有建议这些事情的指导方针，但 Keller & Heckman 合伙人 Greg Kunkle 告诉 The Verge，不太可能有人愿意去检查。

blocks.html_en: “To my knowledge, there’s no real vetting of the US agent,” he tells The Verge. “If you’re making a misstatement, that’s a rule violation, but I don’t think the FCC or TCB is checking those actively. I’m not sure anybody is.”

sentences.en: “To my knowledge, there’s no real vetting of the US agent,” he tells The Verge.
sentences.zh: “据我所知，美国特工没有经过真正的审查，”他告诉 The Verge。

sentences.en: “If you’re making a misstatement, that’s a rule violation, but I don’t think the FCC or TCB is checking those actively.
sentences.zh: “如果你做出错误陈述，那就违反了规则，但我不认为 FCC 或 TCB 正在积极检查这些内容。

sentences.en: I’m not sure anybody is.”
sentences.zh: 我不确定有人是这样。”

blocks.html_en: Kunkle says that for the past decade or so, FCC authorizations haven’t actually been handled by the FCC at all — the agency outsourced that role to roughly 40 private companies called TCBs, short for Telecommunications Certification Bodies. “Once the TCB finishes its process, you’re good to go,” says Kunkle, adding that those authorizations go straight into the FCC database with no more FCC input at all.

sentences.en: Kunkle says that for the past decade or so, FCC authorizations haven’t actually been handled by the FCC at all — the agency outsourced that role to roughly 40 private companies called TCBs, short for Telecommunications Certification Bodies.
sentences.zh: Kunkle 表示，在过去十年左右的时间里，FCC 的授权实际上根本不是由 FCC 处理的——该机构将这一职责外包给了大约 40 家名为 TCB（电信认证机构的缩写）的私营公司。

sentences.en: “Once the TCB finishes its process, you’re good to go,” says Kunkle, adding that those authorizations go straight into the FCC database with no more FCC input at all.
sentences.zh: Kunkle 表示：“一旦 TCB 完成其流程，你就可以开始了。”他补充说，这些授权将直接进入 FCC 数据库，根本不需要 FCC 的任何输入。

blocks.html_en: A TCB could theoretically get in trouble for letting banned companies through, even lose its status — and the FCC does ask TCBs not to take the “US agent” at their word. The agency added specific guidelines this past December about that, including the requirement for “a valid physical US mailing address,” not just a virtual mailbox or a vacant lot:

sentences.en: A TCB could theoretically get in trouble for letting banned companies through, even lose its status — and the FCC does ask TCBs not to take the “US agent” at their word.
sentences.zh: 从理论上讲，TCB 可能会因为允许被禁公司通过而陷入麻烦，甚至失去其地位，而 FCC 确实要求 TCB 不要相信“美国代理人”的话。

sentences.en: The agency added specific guidelines this past December about that, including the requirement for “a valid physical US mailing address,” not just a virtual mailbox or a vacant lot:
sentences.zh: 该机构去年 12 月对此添加了具体指导方针，包括要求“有效的美国实际邮寄地址”，而不仅仅是虚拟邮箱或空地：

sentences.en: The FCC would like TCBs to check if companies are physically located at the address they provide — but it may just be a guideline.
sentences.zh: FCC 希望 TCB 检查公司是否实际位于其提供的地址，但这可能只是一个指导方针。

blocks.html_en: “TCBs should not rely solely on the applicant’s certification,” the FCC writes. If a TCB has any reason to believe that a gadget may be banned, the FCC says it shouldn’t process the application at all. And if a TCB decides it did the wrong thing, it should “immediately submit a KDB inquiry explaining the issue to the FCC,” the agency adds.

sentences.en: “TCBs should not rely solely on the applicant’s certification,” the FCC writes.
sentences.zh: “TCB 不应仅仅依赖申请人的认证，”FCC 写道。

sentences.en: If a TCB has any reason to believe that a gadget may be banned, the FCC says it shouldn’t process the application at all.
sentences.zh: 如果 TCB 有任何理由相信某个设备可能会被禁止，FCC 表示它根本不应该处理该申请。

sentences.en: And if a TCB decides it did the wrong thing, it should “immediately submit a KDB inquiry explaining the issue to the FCC,” the agency adds.
sentences.zh: 该机构补充说，如果 TCB 认为自己做错了事，它应该“立即向 FCC 提交 KDB 调查，解释问题”。

sentences.en: But does that happen?

sentences.en: We reached out to TÜV Rheinland, the TCB that approved Odyssey Robot’s FCC authorization, to let it know we were writing a story about Odyssey Robot making potentially fraudulent attestations, and the company still couldn’t find anything wrong.

sentences.en: If there is a reasonable basis for questioning a certification, we will follow the procedures established by the FCC for such cases,” TÜV Rheinland communications manager Maximilian Grün told The Verge on July 15th.

blocks.html_en: TÜV Rheinland said it hadn’t received any communication from the FCC regarding Odyssey Robot, but also that Odyssey Robot did not forge the TÜV Rheinland test reports attached to its FCC filing. Those were genuine.

blocks.html_en: If the TCBs do their jobs, it’s possible they would catch some fake brands. The TCBs are supposed to audit 5 percent of all devices they certify in a calendar year and report back to the FCC with their findings — including whether a company fails to provide a sample of a product for testing. Perhaps Odyssey Robot would have been the 1 in 20 and get caught that way.

sentences.en: The TCBs are supposed to audit 5 percent of all devices they certify in a calendar year and report back to the FCC with their findings — including whether a company fails to provide a sample of a product for testing.
sentences.zh: TCB 应该审核他们在一个日历年中认证的所有设备的 5%，并向 FCC 报告调查结果，包括公司是否未能提供产品样本进行测试。

blocks.html_en: But so far, the only “DJI front companies” we’ve seen targeted by the FCC are the ones that journalists brought to the agency’s attention.

sentences.en: But so far, the only “DJI front companies” we’ve seen targeted by the FCC are the ones that journalists brought to the agency’s attention.
sentences.zh: 但到目前为止，我们所看到的唯一受到 FCC 攻击的“大疆幌子公司”是记者提请该机构注意的公司。

blocks.html_en: On July 10th, the FCC proposed $25,000 fines against eight of the “DJI front companies,” noting that they were alerted to the brands through research from Konrad Iturbe and my own coverage on The Verge. It justified the fines, and possibly further action, because those companies’ US agents didn’t even bother replying to the agency’s letters.

sentences.en: On July 10th, the FCC proposed $25,000 fines against eight of the “DJI front companies,” noting that they were alerted to the brands through research from Konrad Iturbe and my own coverage on The Verge.
sentences.zh: 7 月 10 日，FCC 提议对 8 家“DJI 幌子公司”处以 25,000 美元的罚款，并指出他们通过 Konrad Iturbe 的研究和我自己在 The Verge 上的报道对这些品牌发出了警告。

sentences.en: It justified the fines, and possibly further action, because those companies’ US agents didn’t even bother replying to the agency’s letters.
sentences.zh: 它证明了罚款以及可能采取的进一步行动是合理的，因为这些公司的美国代理人甚至懒得回复该机构的信件。

blocks.html_en: In 2024, the FCC similarly proposed over $700,000 in fines against Eken, a Hong Kong-based video doorbell manufacturer that also failed to provide working contact information for a US agent. That time, it was journalists at Consumer Reports who prompted the FCC to act.

sentences.en: In 2024, the FCC similarly proposed over $700,000 in fines against Eken, a Hong Kong-based video doorbell manufacturer that also failed to provide working contact information for a US agent.
sentences.zh: 2024 年，FCC 类似地对总部位于香港的视频门铃制造商 Eken 处以超过 70 万美元的罚款，该公司也未能提供美国特工的工作联系信息。

sentences.en: That time, it was journalists at Consumer Reports who prompted the FCC to act.
sentences.zh: 当时，是《消费者报告》的记者促使 FCC 采取行动。

blocks.html_en: But even if the FCC can issue a fine, it probably won’t be able to collect.

sentences.en: But even if the FCC can issue a fine, it probably won’t be able to collect.
sentences.zh: 但即使 FCC 可以开罚单，也可能收不到钱。

blocks.html_en: Earlier this year, the agency cited the Eken case as evidence that “foreign actors in our equipment authorization have been able to evade US law when products they are responsible for have harmed American consumers.” Even if a company pretends to be based in the US, the FCC can’t reach them if they’re actually in China instead.

sentences.en: Earlier this year, the agency cited the Eken case as evidence that “foreign actors in our equipment authorization have been able to evade US law when products they are responsible for have harmed American consumers.”
sentences.zh: 今年早些时候，该机构援引埃肯案作为证据，证明“我们的设备授权中的外国行为者在其负责的产品伤害了美国消费者时能够逃避美国法律。”

sentences.en: Even if a company pretends to be based in the US, the FCC can’t reach them if they’re actually in China instead.
sentences.zh: 即使一家公司假装总部位于美国，如果他们实际上位于中国，联邦通信委员会也无法联系到他们。

blocks.html_en: There might still be real financial consequences for some DJI front companies. On July 17th, the FCC took things up a notch by threatening to retroactively ban the DJI fronts’ already-approved products from import, sale, marketing, and distribution, which could be the end for successful brands like Xtra and Skyrover that snuck in before the ban.

sentences.en: There might still be real financial consequences for some DJI front companies.
sentences.zh: 对于一些大疆幌子公司来说，可能仍然会产生真正的财务后果。

sentences.en: On July 17th, the FCC took things up a notch by threatening to retroactively ban the DJI fronts’ already-approved products from import, sale, marketing, and distribution, which could be the end for successful brands like Xtra and Skyrover that snuck in before the ban.
sentences.zh: 7 月 17 日，FCC 的态度更上一层楼，威胁要追溯性地禁止大疆旗下已经批准的产品进口、销售、营销和分销，这可能是 Xtra 和 Skyrover 等在禁令颁布之前就潜入的成功品牌的终结。

blocks.html_en: But it seems like that might be where the FCC’s direct powers of enforcement end. Former FCC enforcement chief Peter Hyun tells The Verge that the agency will want to send a message to other companies not to do this thing, but the tools it has in its tool belt are forfeitures, cease and desist letters, and “show-cause” orders where a company has to explain why its license shouldn’t be revoked. The FCC could also theoretically help the Department of Justice bring a smuggling charge, he says.

sentences.en: But it seems like that might be where the FCC’s direct powers of enforcement end.
sentences.zh: 但联邦通信委员会的直接执法权力似乎就到此为止了。

sentences.en: Former FCC enforcement chief Peter Hyun tells The Verge that the agency will want to send a message to other companies not to do this thing, but the tools it has in its tool belt are forfeitures, cease and desist letters, and “show-cause” orders where a company has to explain why its license shouldn’t be revoked.
sentences.zh: 前 FCC 执法负责人 Peter Hyun 告诉 The Verge，该机构希望向其他公司发出不要这样做的信息，但其工具箱中拥有的工具包括没收、停止和终止信函以及“说明原因”命令，公司必须解释为什么不应吊销其许可证。

sentences.en: The FCC could also theoretically help the Department of Justice bring a smuggling charge, he says.
sentences.zh: 他说，理论上，联邦通信委员会还可以帮助司法部提出走私指控。

blocks.html_en: Lying smugglers hiding behind disconnected phone numbers won’t be stepping forward to provide their real identities, of course.

sentences.en: Lying smugglers hiding behind disconnected phone numbers won’t be stepping forward to provide their real identities, of course.
sentences.zh: 当然，隐藏在断开的电话号码后面的说谎的走私者不会站出来提供他们的真实身份。

blocks.html_en: On July 21st, a month and a half after Iturbe and The Verge asked the FCC about Odyssey Robot for this story, the agency revealed it had sent a “show-cause” order to the company a few days after we tipped them off. The FCC’s investigation was limited to sending letters to Odyssey and eTak and noting whether the two entities responded. Odyssey didn’t respond. eTak denied any relationship with Odyssey.

sentences.en: On July 21st, a month and a half after Iturbe and The Verge asked the FCC about Odyssey Robot for this story, the agency revealed it had sent a “show-cause” order to the company a few days after we tipped them off.
sentences.zh: 7 月 21 日，也就是 Iturbe 和 The Verge 向 FCC 询问有关 Odyssey Robot 的报道的一个半月后，该机构透露，在我们向 FCC 举报几天后，它已向该公司发出了“展示原因”命令。

sentences.en: The FCC’s investigation was limited to sending letters to Odyssey and eTak and noting whether the two entities responded.
sentences.zh: FCC 的调查仅限于向 Odyssey 和 eTak 发送信件，并注意这两个实体是否做出回应。

sentences.en: Odyssey didn’t respond.
sentences.zh: 奥德赛没有回应。

sentences.en: eTak denied any relationship with Odyssey.
sentences.zh: eTak 否认与奥德赛有任何关系。

blocks.html_en: eTak replied to the FCC on July 13th, the same day I spoke to its founder on the phone.

sentences.en: eTak replied to the FCC on July 13th, the same day I spoke to its founder on the phone.
sentences.zh: eTak 于 7 月 13 日回复了 FCC，同一天我与其创始人通了电话。

blocks.html_en: The question now isn’t what happens to the empty husk of Odyssey Robot, but how many other smugglers might sneak through when the agency isn’t tipped off. If the only reason the FCC caught Odyssey is a guy named Konrad — well, that’s not how government agencies are supposed to work.

sentences.en: The question now isn’t what happens to the empty husk of Odyssey Robot, but how many other smugglers might sneak through when the agency isn’t tipped off.
sentences.zh: 现在的问题不是奥德赛机器人的空壳会发生什么，而是在没有向该机构通报情况的情况下，有多少其他走私者可能会偷偷溜进去。

sentences.en: If the only reason the FCC caught Odyssey is a guy named Konrad — well, that’s not how government agencies are supposed to work.
sentences.zh: 如果联邦通信委员会抓住奥德赛的唯一原因是一个名叫康拉德的人——那么，这不是政府机构应该如何工作的。

blocks.html_en: The FCC’s consumer gadget bans are broken if they don’t require anyone to do a basic Google search before legally approving banned gadgets for import and sale. They’re broken if the FCC can’t track down and make a meaningful example of a bad actor even after they’re caught, because the same lies that made them invisible also shield them from consequences.

sentences.en: The FCC’s consumer gadget bans are broken if they don’t require anyone to do a basic Google search before legally approving banned gadgets for import and sale.
sentences.zh: 如果联邦通信委员会的消费电子产品禁令不要求任何人在合法批准进口和销售被禁止的电子产品之前进行基本的谷歌搜索，那么它们就被打破了。

sentences.en: They’re broken if the FCC can’t track down and make a meaningful example of a bad actor even after they’re caught, because the same lies that made them invisible also shield them from consequences.
sentences.zh: 如果联邦通信委员会即使在被抓到之后也无法追查不良行为者并为他们树立一个有意义的榜样，那么他们就会崩溃，因为让他们隐形的同样的谎言也让他们免受后果。

blocks.html_en: Since Odyssey hadn’t yet begun selling drones in the US, revoking its FCC authorization likely wouldn’t matter to its mystery owners. It wouldn’t be surprising if their takeaway is to lie more carefully, rather than not lying at all.

sentences.en: Since Odyssey hadn’t yet begun selling drones in the US, revoking its FCC authorization likely wouldn’t matter to its mystery owners.
sentences.zh: 由于奥德赛尚未开始在美国销售无人机，因此撤销其 FCC 授权对其神秘所有者来说可能并不重要。

sentences.en: It wouldn’t be surprising if their takeaway is to lie more carefully, rather than not lying at all.
sentences.zh: 如果他们的收获是更加小心地撒谎，而不是根本不撒谎，那就不足为奇了。

blocks.html_en: But it does seem that the FCC is attempting to close some loopholes.

sentences.en: But it does seem that the FCC is attempting to close some loopholes.
sentences.zh: 但联邦通信委员会似乎确实正在试图堵住一些漏洞。

blocks.html_en: At first, it seemed that the FCC was following the America First formula of punishing Chinese entities based on their affiliation rather than their actions — it took action against eight so-called “bad labs” that test radios in China simply because Chinese state-sponsored entities had more than 10 percent equity. When it moved against SGS-CSTC Shenzhen, a lab used by at least one DJI front, it didn’t mention that front in its justification for the move.

sentences.en: At first, it seemed that the FCC was following the America First formula of punishing Chinese entities based on their affiliation rather than their actions — it took action against eight so-called “bad labs” that test radios in China simply because Chinese state-sponsored entities had more than 10 percent equity.
sentences.zh: 起初，联邦通信委员会似乎遵循美国优先的模式，根据隶属关系而不是行为来惩罚中国实体——它对八个在中国测试无线电的所谓“不良实验室”采取了行动，仅仅是因为中国国家资助的实体拥有超过 10% 的股权。

sentences.en: When it moved against SGS-CSTC Shenzhen, a lab used by at least one DJI front, it didn’t mention that front in its justification for the move.
sentences.zh: 当它针对 SGS-CSTC 深圳实验室（该实验室被至少一个大疆创新阵线使用）时，它在其此举的理由中没有提及该阵线。

blocks.html_en: But on July 21st, the FCC moved against test lab Shenzhen STS because it “willfully provided false test results” for a variety of Bluetooth headsets, phones, and tablets, which the lab blamed on negligent staff. Perhaps the FCC will act against TCBs and test labs that aren’t carefully checking banned gadgets, too.

sentences.zh: 也许 FCC 也会对那些不仔细检查违禁设备的 TCB 和测试实验室采取行动。

blocks.html_en: The agency is also now proposing to make online retailers like Amazon display the FCC IDs of each device on their product pages so that consumers can check for themselves whether the names line up, at least. blocks.html_en: The FCC did not respond to any of our repeated requests for comment over the past two months.

sentences.en: The FCC did not respond to any of our repeated requests for comment over the past two months.
sentences.zh: 过去两个月，美国联邦通信委员会 (FCC) 没有回应我们多次提出的置评请求。

blocks.html_en: So far, we’ve been unable to track down “Randolph Howard Eason,” the “compliance director” who submitted Odyssey Robot’s paperwork. No one we’ve found by that name or similar seems likely to be a fit, and those who might have been paid by Odyssey — like the coworking space and TÜV Rheinland — declined to tell The Verge anything more about their clients.

He says he processes trademark filings for all sorts of Chinese companies as a side gig, getting his assignments from a Chinese firm that charges a flat fee for US trademarks.

blocks.html_en: “I hate to say it, but it would be pretty easy,” says Kunkle. “I think just the volume of devices coming into the United States — if you’re the type of person that’s willing to fudge this paperwork and you reside in some place where you think you’re unreachable by the US government, I don’t think this would be hard to fake at all.”

blocks.html_en: • Sean Hollister

sentences.en: Sean Hollister
sentences.zh: 肖恩·霍利斯特

content_html_zh:
美国试图禁止中国无人机。 布伦丹·卡尔无法让他们脚踏实地。

通过肖恩·霍利斯特

世界标准时间 2026 年 7 月 24 日中午 12:00

肖恩·霍利斯特

是 The Verge 的高级编辑和创始成员，涵盖小玩意、游戏和玩具。 他花了 15 年时间编辑 CNET、Gizmodo 和 Engadget 等网站。

在加利福尼亚州帕萨迪纳，有一个可爱的红砖庭院，其中一个店面与其他店面不同。 玻璃门通向一条稀疏的工业走廊，通向阳光明媚的门厅，门厅设有大型螺旋楼梯。 上去，你会看到一个典型的联合办公空间：开放的桌子、健康的零食、一系列带有玻璃墙的会议室。 沿着一条走廊走完大部分路程，您就会找到 Office 18。

这间狭小的私人办公室本应是美国无人机公司奥德赛机器人的总部，但没有任何生命迹象。 我们看到了三张小桌椅，但没有电源板，没有显示器，没有键盘，没有充电器，也没有装饰品。 其中两张桌子上覆盖着密封的纸板包装。

这个房间就是最新的证据：FCC 对外国制造的无人机的禁令是一个糟糕的笑话。

2025 年 12 月 22 日，美国政府在没有任何公开证据的情况下声称，外国消费级无人机对国家安全构成了不可接受的威胁。 在其他机构的帮助下，联邦通信委员会主席布伦丹·卡尔禁止未来所有外国无人机进入该国，而无需获得特别的新豁免。

就像卡尔提出的有意义的消费者互联网路由器禁令一样，这项豁免与这些产品的安全性无关。 该应用程序包括关于限制或改进技术本身的零问题。

相反，它赋予卡尔和唐纳德·特朗普总统决定输家和赢家的权力，同时敲响“美国优先”的鼓点。 外国公司必须提交一份在美国生产产品的详细计划，包括他们打算在美国制造业投资多少——然而，总部位于美国的 Netgear 获得了路由器禁令的豁免，尽管它没有承诺在亚洲以外生产设备。

消费级无人机实际上全部在中国制造，截至 4 月份，似乎没有人计划因 FCC 的外国无人机禁令而改变这一现状。 尽管存在一些美国无人机制造商，但他们正在追求一种不同的、更有利可图的机会：侦察和杀戮的无人机。

但 Office 18 显示了 FCC 计划中的另一个漏洞。 它没有创建新的美国消费无人机公司，而是激励假冒公司。

欺骗 FCC 有多容易？ 看起来只是在文书工作上撒谎。

只要创办一家新公司，假装在美国制造全新的无人机，并且指望没有人注意到——即使你的无人机明显使用专有的 DJI 无线技术。

全球最大的无人机公司大疆创新并不仅仅因为先发优势而在无人机领域占据主导地位。 这是技术。 DJI 的 OcuSync 和较旧的 Lightbridge 实时视频传输系统过去和现在都是远程控制消费级无人机的最可靠的远程、低延迟方式。 几乎每次 The Verge 测试另一个品牌时，信号都不太可靠。 这是大疆不会轻易分享的竞争优势。

但 OcuSync 有一个明显的指纹。 它使用特定的无线电频率对，这些频率对出现在 FCC 的数据库中。

当软件开发人员兼记者 Konrad Iturbe 开始查看 FCC 数据库中的这些频率时，他意识到 DJI 正准备在美国玩一场盛大的打地鼠游戏。 早在 2025 年 12 月无人机禁令出台之前，许多新公司突然出现，销售几乎不加掩饰的 DJI 技术版本。 他称它们为“大疆幌子公司”。

Skyrover 是进入美国的最著名的 DJI 克隆产品之一，该公司发布了一款与 DJI Mini 4 Pro 非常相似的廉价无人机。 去年 7 月，DJI 没有向 The Verge 证实或否认“Skyrover”是其自己无人机的伪装版本，尽管安全研究人员证实其应用程序包含 DJI 代码。

然后，在 10 月份，Iturbe 帮助我们向您介绍了 Xtra，这家公司让 DJI 将其受欢迎的相机偷偷进入美国。 当我们测试 Xtra 相机与 DJI 相机时，我们发现它们非常相似，这消除了任何疑虑。 再次强调，大疆创新既没有证实也没有否认。

结果，美国公民仍然能够购买大疆科技——只是现在，他们正在处理不可靠的业务，未来没有真正的支持保证。

今年六月，伊图尔韦发现了迄今为止最无耻的尝试。 DJI 指纹让他找到了一家名为 Odyssey Robot 的公司向 FCC 提交的文件，该公司的业务名称为 Galiview Tech。 今年3月，它成为自无人机禁令开始以来第一个获得FCC批准的配备DJI的无人机品牌。 大疆创新没有回复对此报道的置评请求。

如果说 Galiview 试图隐藏 DJI 连接，那么它并没有非常努力：该公司的网站甚至将 DJI 的商标 OcuSync 技术列为 Galiview 功能。

阻止秘密的中国硬件难道不是禁令的全部目的吗？ FCC 如何批准这款无人机？

很简单：2 月 6 日，“Randolph Howard Eason”在 FCC 表格上宣称，Odyssey Robot 的无人机和控制器是在美国制造的，在批准其申请之前，没有人质疑这一点。

FCC 可能发现了 OcuSync 频率，或者这个据称是美国制造的小工具由中国实验室进行了测试，或者它使用了另一架中国无人机也使用的中国制造的天线。

但这比那更容易。 奥德赛机器人宣称，其无人机是在加利福尼亚州帕萨迪纳市米勒巷21号210室设计、开发和制造的。 即使是基本的谷歌搜索也可以告诉你这不是一家工厂的地址——这是一个名为“Industrious”的联合办公空间，明确禁止成员在现场制造任何东西。

奥德赛机器人还宣称，其无人机是在德克萨斯州大草原城的 eTak Worldwide Corporation 组装的。 拥有 80,000 平方英尺的仓库空间和 15 个装卸码头，理论上您可以在那里建造无人机。 但同样，基本的 Google 搜索会告诉您 eTak 不是汇编程序；它是汇编程序。这是一家回收公司，收集电子垃圾，包括旧电池，然后对其进行分类和拆除。

尽管如此，我们还是想确定一下。 我们打电话给帕萨迪纳联合办公空间的接待员；他们证实奥德赛机器人正在积极租用一个两人私人办公室。 加州官方商业记录称该公司在 Office 18 之外运营。 剩下的事情你都知道了：我们发现了一个孤独的房间，里面有三张未使用的桌子，上面放着纸板箱。

如果联邦通信委员会曾经看过这些无人机的网站，它可能会发现更多令人惊讶的矛盾。 该网站声称“Galiview Inc.”成立于 2022 年，总部位于旧金山，尽管它提供了拉斯维加斯的电话号码和帕萨迪纳的地址。

它声称：“我们位于旧金山技术创新中心的中心，一直在突破消费级无人机技术的界限。”

事实上，公共记录显示 Galiview 并不是一家公司。 这是 Odyssey Robot, LLC 的商标，该公司于 2025 年 1 月在特拉华州成立，去年 12 月在加利福尼亚州注册，此前曾尝试将品牌命名为“Flyon”，但似乎失败了。 2026 年 2 月提交的其他商标申请显示，该公司正在规划至少三种产品：Galiview Titan、Galiview Venus 和 Galiview Nova。

该公司从未回复我发送给 contact@flyondrone.com 的电子邮件，这是该公司公开提供的唯一一个电子邮件地址。 我们发现它唯一的电话号码已断开：“您拨打的用户不在服务区。”

回收商 eTak Worldwide 也没有回复电子邮件或接听电话。 “抱歉，该用户的邮箱无法再接受邮件了。”我一次又一次听到这样的声音。 我们的运气也好不到“iNet Expert”，这是一家由 eTak 创始人创立的早期公司，声称提供批发电脑零件。 每次我尝试该公司的电话树时，得到的答案总是机械式的“无人接听”。

但当我意识到 iNet Expert 距离我家只有 30 分钟路程时，我决定去参观一下。

大楼锁着，但透过窗户我看到了一些奇怪的东西。 主要装饰是 2014 年一款智能手机屏幕保护膜的几乎从地板到天花板的营销图片，来自一家名为 Rudaor 的公司。 从 YouTube 上大约 300 次的观看次数来看，iNet 似乎试图成为美国发行商。 iNet 办公室的窗户上还挂着 Hirutech USA 的标志，该公司是一家台湾电子业务开发公司已停业的美国分公司，曾经占据同一地址。

1/3iNet Expert 标志看起来很旧。

整座建筑就像一个时间胶囊——然而，根据 ImportYeti 收集的进口记录，它仍然收到一些来自海外的货物，包括 7 月 15 日的货物。

我想，eTak 的创始人有出借他的公司地址的历史。

但当我最终偶然发现 eTak 和 iNet Expert 创始人 Richard Lee 的手机号码时，他说他直到一周左右才听说过 Odyssey Robot。

“我很高兴你给我打电话，因为我们完全不知道发生了什么事，”李在 7 月 13 日告诉我。 “为什么人们要用我们公司的名义来做这种事？ 这有点荒谬。”

当我向他展示奥德赛机器人关于 eTak 组装无人机的宣誓证明时，他说 eTak 一定是从搜索引擎中挑选了他的地址。

当我询问时，他说道：“不，我们不组装任何东西，我们只进行翻新。”并补充说 eTak 没有人力。 他说，他为台湾电脑制造商纬创资通（该公司为惠普和戴尔等巨头生产笔记本电脑）回收电池，为 Anker 回收 UV 墨盒，为科沃斯回收吸尘器，但他从未与无人机制造商大疆建立过合作关系。

“他们是一家大公司，他们不会选择我们，”他说。 “如果他们使用我们公司的名称，这对我们来说不公平，也不正确。”

但无论奥德赛机器人背后的人是谁，都可能逃脱谎言。

自 2023 年以来，理论上每家在美国销售无线设备的外国公司都需要一名驻扎在美国的额外人员参与其中。 那年 2 月，FCC 增加了对“美国代理人”的要求，FCC 可以合法地向其送达文件并与其进行沟通，这样 FCC 就可以对根据国际法可能无法联系到的公司采取强制措施。

但规则中没有任何内容要求任何人验证美国特工是一位知名的、信誉良好的人，拥有工作电话号码和电子邮件地址，在实际的常设办公室工作。 尽管 FCC 可能有建议这些事情的指导方针，但 Keller & Heckman 合伙人 Greg Kunkle 告诉 The Verge，不太可能有人愿意去检查。

“据我所知，美国特工没有经过真正的审查，”他告诉 The Verge。 “如果你做出错误陈述，那就违反了规则，但我不认为 FCC 或 TCB 正在积极检查这些内容。 我不确定有人是这样。”

Kunkle 表示，在过去十年左右的时间里，FCC 的授权实际上根本不是由 FCC 处理的——该机构将这一职责外包给了大约 40 家名为 TCB（电信认证机构的缩写）的私营公司。 Kunkle 表示：“一旦 TCB 完成其流程，你就可以开始了。”他补充说，这些授权将直接进入 FCC 数据库，根本不需要 FCC 的任何输入。

从理论上讲，TCB 可能会因为允许被禁公司通过而陷入麻烦，甚至失去其地位，而 FCC 确实要求 TCB 不要相信“美国代理人”的话。 该机构去年 12 月对此添加了具体指导方针，包括要求“有效的美国实际邮寄地址”，而不仅仅是虚拟邮箱或空地：

“TCB 不应仅仅依赖申请人的认证，”FCC 写道。 如果 TCB 有任何理由相信某个设备可能会被禁止，FCC 表示它根本不应该处理该申请。 该机构补充说，如果 TCB 认为自己做错了事，它应该“立即向 FCC 提交 KDB 调查，解释问题”。

但这种情况真的会发生吗？ 我们联系了 TÜV Rheinland（批准 Odyssey Robot FCC 授权的 TCB），让其知道我们正在撰写一篇有关 Odyssey Robot 进行潜在欺诈证明的报道，而该公司仍然找不到任何问题。

“截至今天，我们没有任何具体证据来质疑我们对奥德赛机器人的认证。 如果对认证提出质疑有合理依据，我们将遵循 FCC 针对此类案件制定的程序。”TÜV Rheinland 公关经理 Maximilian Grün 于 7 月 15 日告诉 The Verge。

TÜV莱茵表示，尚未收到FCC关于Odyssey Robot的任何信息，而且Odyssey Robot并未伪造其FCC文件中所附的TÜV Rheinland测试报告。 那些都是真品。

如果 TCB 尽职尽责，他们可能会抓到一些假冒品牌。 TCB 应该审核他们在一个日历年中认证的所有设备的 5%，并向 FCC 报告调查结果，包括公司是否未能提供产品样本进行测试。 也许奥德赛机器人是 20 个机器人中第一个那样被抓到的。

但到目前为止，我们所看到的唯一受到 FCC 攻击的“大疆幌子公司”是记者提请该机构注意的公司。

7 月 10 日，FCC 提议对 8 家“DJI 幌子公司”处以 25,000 美元的罚款，并指出他们通过 Konrad Iturbe 的研究和我自己在 The Verge 上的报道对这些品牌发出了警告。 它证明了罚款以及可能采取的进一步行动是合理的，因为这些公司的美国代理人甚至懒得回复该机构的信件。

2024 年，FCC 类似地对总部位于香港的视频门铃制造商 Eken 处以超过 70 万美元的罚款，该公司也未能提供美国特工的工作联系信息。 当时，是《消费者报告》的记者促使 FCC 采取行动。

但即使 FCC 可以开罚单，也可能收不到钱。

今年早些时候，该机构援引埃肯案作为证据，证明“我们的设备授权中的外国行为者在其负责的产品伤害了美国消费者时能够逃避美国法律。” 即使一家公司假装总部位于美国，如果他们实际上位于中国，联邦通信委员会也无法联系到他们。

对于一些大疆幌子公司来说，可能仍然会产生真正的财务后果。 7 月 17 日，FCC 的态度更上一层楼，威胁要追溯性地禁止大疆旗下已经批准的产品进口、销售、营销和分销，这可能是 Xtra 和 Skyrover 等在禁令颁布之前就潜入的成功品牌的终结。

但联邦通信委员会的直接执法权力似乎就到此为止了。 前 FCC 执法负责人 Peter Hyun 告诉 The Verge，该机构希望向其他公司发出不要这样做的信息，但其工具箱中拥有的工具包括没收、停止和终止信函以及“说明原因”命令，公司必须解释为什么不应吊销其许可证。 他说，理论上，联邦通信委员会还可以帮助司法部提出走私指控。

当然，隐藏在断开的电话号码后面的说谎的走私者不会站出来提供他们的真实身份。

7 月 21 日，也就是 Iturbe 和 The Verge 向 FCC 询问有关 Odyssey Robot 的报道的一个半月后，该机构透露，在我们向 FCC 举报几天后，它已向该公司发出了“展示原因”命令。 FCC 的调查仅限于向 Odyssey 和 eTak 发送信件，并注意这两个实体是否做出回应。 奥德赛没有回应。 eTak 否认与奥德赛有任何关系。

eTak 于 7 月 13 日回复了 FCC，同一天我与其创始人通了电话。

现在的问题不是奥德赛机器人的空壳会发生什么，而是在没有向该机构通报情况的情况下，有多少其他走私者可能会偷偷溜进去。 如果联邦通信委员会抓住奥德赛的唯一原因是一个名叫康拉德的人——那么，这不是政府机构应该如何工作的。

如果联邦通信委员会的消费电子产品禁令不要求任何人在合法批准进口和销售被禁止的电子产品之前进行基本的谷歌搜索，那么它们就被打破了。 如果联邦通信委员会即使在被抓到之后也无法追查不良行为者并为他们树立一个有意义的榜样，那么他们就会崩溃，因为让他们隐形的同样的谎言也让他们免受后果。

由于奥德赛尚未开始在美国销售无人机，因此撤销其 FCC 授权对其神秘所有者来说可能并不重要。 如果他们的收获是更加小心地撒谎，而不是根本不撒谎，那就不足为奇了。

但联邦通信委员会似乎确实正在试图堵住一些漏洞。

起初，联邦通信委员会似乎遵循美国优先的模式，根据隶属关系而不是行为来惩罚中国实体——它对八个在中国测试无线电的所谓“不良实验室”采取了行动，仅仅是因为中国国家资助的实体拥有超过 10% 的股权。 当它针对 SGS-CSTC 深圳实验室（该实验室被至少一个大疆创新阵线使用）时，它在其此举的理由中没有提及该阵线。

但7月21日，FCC对深圳STS测试实验室采取了行动，因为该实验室为多款蓝牙耳机、手机和平板电脑“故意提供虚假测试结果”，实验室将此归咎于工作人员的疏忽。 也许 FCC 也会对那些不仔细检查违禁设备的 TCB 和测试实验室采取行动。

该机构现在还提议让亚马逊等在线零售商在其产品页面上显示每台设备的 FCC ID，以便消费者至少可以自行检查名称是否一致。 亚马逊已经从卖家那里收集了这些 FCC ID，但发言人 Juliana Karber 证实该公司从未要求他们公开展示这些 ID。

过去两个月，美国联邦通信委员会 (FCC) 没有回应我们多次提出的置评请求。

到目前为止，我们还无法找到提交奥德赛机器人文件的“合规总监”“伦道夫·霍华德·伊森”。 我们发现没有一个叫这个名字或类似名字的人可能适合，而那些可能由奥德赛支付的人——比如联合办公空间和 TÜV Rheinland——拒绝告诉 The Verge 关于他们客户的更多信息。

并不是说我们一定能走得很远，因为奥德赛机器人利用中间人来掩盖其踪迹。 例如，处理“Galiview”商标的夏威夷律师肖恩·哈特利布 (Sean Hartlieb) 告诉 The Verge，他从未与该公司进行过任何互动。 他说，他为各种中国公司处理商标申请是一种副业，他的任务是从一家对美国商标收取固定费用的中国公司获得的。

通过支付少量的标准化费用，对于一家大公司来说，建立大量空壳公司并看看哪些公司逃过注意可能不会那么昂贵。 “20,000 美元，大概是这样吧？” 昆克尔表示，每一项都可能要付出代价。 “商标、TCB、测试实验室，可能都在这个范围内。 仅此而已。”

那么，是什么阻止了国外大品牌的潜入呢？

“我不想这么说，但这很容易，”昆克尔说。 “我认为进入美国的设备数量——如果你是那种愿意伪造这些文件的人，并且你居住在一个你认为美国政府无法联系到你的地方，我认为这根本不难伪造。”

如果有人要在奥德赛机器人和大疆之间建立明确的联系，我认为这将是在美国注册有限责任公司的公司之一。 奥德赛机器人目前已通过特拉华州商业公司和加利福尼亚州注册代理注册。 但特拉华州允许公司匿名设立有限责任公司。

“不幸的是，这为欺诈打开了大门，而且没有太多追索权，”特拉华商业公司的接待员说。

加州注册代理的接待员告诉我，如果我想联系奥德赛机器人，我可以尝试将消息邮寄到她在萨克拉门托的办公室。

“我们是真的！” 她说。 “不幸的是，有时我们的客户并非如此。”
